name: First National Bank Of South Carolina
description_short: First National Bank of South Carolina is a community bank founded in 1905 offering checking, savings, loans, and financial services across eight South Carolina locations.
description_long:
First National Bank of South Carolina traces its origins to July 7, 1905, when The Bank of Holly Hill was chartered in the rural farming community of Holly Hill. The bank survived the 1907 town fire, the 1929 stock market crash that closed over 5,000 banks nationwide, and President Roosevelt's 1933 bank closures—distinguishing itself as one of the few institutions deemed sound enough to remain operational during these crises. This longevity reflects the bank's conservative financial management and deep community roots.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking services. Personal products include checking accounts, savings accounts, Roo Crew Savings (youth accounts), certificates of deposit, IRAs, and health savings accounts. For business customers, they provide business checking, business savings, CDs, and merchant services. Lending services span commercial loans, personal loans, and mortgage loans. Additional financial services include VISA check cards, traveler's checks, safe deposit boxes, ATM access at all branches, and Zelle money transfer capabilities.

FNBSC distinguishes itself through over 100 years of continuous operation and deep historical ties to South Carolina communities. The bank maintains a personalized approach with named loan officers available to meet customers, publishes current interest rates transparently, and offers financial calculators to help customers make informed decisions. Their eight-location branch network and ATM availability provide accessibility, while their mobile app extends digital convenience.

As a regional community bank, FNBSC offers stability and local decision-making but likely cannot match the product breadth or aggressive rates of large national banks or online-only institutions. The website provides limited detail on specific APRs, fees, or minimum balance requirements, requiring direct contact for pricing information. For customers prioritizing community banking relationships and local service over cutting-edge digital features or nationally competitive rates, FNBSC represents a viable option. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Over 118 years of continuous operation, surviving the Great Depression and multiple financial crises
Eight branch locations across South Carolina with ATMs at every location
Named loan officers available for personalized financial consultation
Comprehensive product suite covering personal, business, and lending needs in one institution
Zelle money transfer integration for modern digital payments
Publishes current interest rates transparently for customer comparison
Offers both traditional products (safe deposit boxes, traveler's checks) and modern services (mobile app, financial calculators)
cons: Limited geographic footprint (8 locations) restricts access for customers outside South Carolina service areas
Website provides no specific APRs, fees, or minimum deposit requirements—requires phone contact (803-496-5011) for pricing
No mention of competitive promotional rates or sign-up bonuses typical of modern banks
Limited transparency on digital banking features, online account opening, or app functionality beyond mention of availability
best_for: South Carolina residents seeking relationship-based community banking with local branch access
Business owners preferring personalized service and local decision-making over large corporate banking
Customers valuing institutional stability and long-term community presence over cutting-edge digital features
Individuals and businesses wanting comprehensive banking services (checking, savings, loans, merchant services) from one regional institution
diagnosis: First National Bank of South Carolina is best for South Carolina residents and businesses seeking personalized community banking with institutional longevity and comprehensive services. The main caveat is limited geographic availability outside South Carolina and lack of transparent online pricing, requiring direct contact to compare rates and fees.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
